questions.prompt: Which is the strongest evidence that a source might be biased?
choices: The article includes dates and names.
The author sells the product they praise and never mentions drawbacks.
The website ends in .org.
There are three paragraphs.
questions.answer: The author sells the product they praise and never mentions drawbacks.

questions.prompt: Read: "Because the bus was late, we missed the opening scene." The word "Because" signals —
choices: a cause-and-effect relationship.
a question to the reader
a list of unrelated facts
the story is fictional

questions.prompt: A flyer says, "Nine out of ten dentists in our survey recommend BrightSmile!" What should a careful reader ask first?
choices: Whether the flyer uses colour ink.
How many words are on the flyer.
If dentists exist in other countries.
How those dentists were chosen and whether the survey was fair.
questions.answer: How those dentists were chosen and whether the survey was fair.

questions.prompt: Which conclusion follows logically? All robins in this park have red breasts. This bird in the park has no red breast.
choices: It must be an eagle.
All birds in the park are robins.
It is not a robin (at least not one matching that description).
Robins never visit parks.
questions.answer: It is not a robin (at least not one matching that description).

questions.prompt: "She revised her draft three times before submitting." This detail mainly suggests she —
choices: dislikes writing completely
cares about quality and improving her work.
never reads instructions
finished in under one minute
questions.answer: cares about quality and improving her work.

questions.prompt: Which pair shows a fact (not an opinion)?
choices: Lake Ontario is one of the Great Lakes.
Lake Ontario is the prettiest lake.
Summer is the best season.
Chocolate ice cream is tastier than vanilla.
questions.answer: Lake Ontario is one of the Great Lakes.

questions.prompt: A student argues, "We should start school later because teenagers need more sleep." Which statement is a counterargument?
choices: Teenagers are human beings.
Sleep happens at night.
Schools have buildings.
Later start times can make sports practices and after-school jobs harder to schedule.
questions.answer: Later start times can make sports practices and after-school jobs harder to schedule.

choices: The fog rolled in before dawn.
The harbour was closed Tuesday.
The fog was like a thick blanket over the harbour.
Blankets are sold at the store.
questions.answer: The fog was like a thick blanket over the harbour.

questions.prompt: Two friends disagree whether a movie is "good." What makes their disagreement hard to settle with one proof?
choices: Movies always last exactly two hours.
"Good" is a value judgment and people use different criteria.
Theatre seats are made of fabric.
Popcorn is salty.
questions.answer: "Good" is a value judgment and people use different criteria.

questions.prompt: If everyone in town who answers a poll shops at the same store that day, the results likely —
choices: overrepresent shoppers at that store and may not reflect the whole town.
are perfectly random
must be ignored forever
only count people over 100 years old
questions.answer: overrepresent shoppers at that store and may not reflect the whole town.

questions.prompt: Which question best checks whether a claim is reasonable?
choices: How many letters are in the claim?
Was the claim written in cursive?
Does the claim rhyme?
What evidence supports it, and is that evidence trustworthy?
questions.answer: What evidence supports it, and is that evidence trustworthy?

questions.prompt: "Although the hike was long, the view was worth it." The contrast set up by "Although" emphasizes —
choices: that hiking is illegal
that views never matter
that the positive outcome mattered despite the difficulty.
that time always stands still
questions.answer: that the positive outcome mattered despite the difficulty.

questions.prompt: A headline reads, "New study: screens harm all children." A critical reader should notice —
choices: headlines are always exactly 10 words
words like "all" are extreme and may not match the actual study.
studies never use data
children never use screens
questions.answer: words like "all" are extreme and may not match the actual study.

questions.prompt: Which is an example of a false cause fallacy?
choices: The rooster crows, then the sun rises; therefore the rooster causes sunrise.
Water boils at 100 °C at sea level.
If it rains, the ground gets wet.
A square has four equal sides.
questions.answer: The rooster crows, then the sun rises; therefore the rooster causes sunrise.

questions.prompt: When comparing two products online, which practice is most fair?
choices: Trust only the shortest description.
Choose the one with the brightest logo.
Ignore reviews written on weekdays.
Use the same criteria for both (price, warranty, reviews) and check multiple sources.
questions.answer: Use the same criteria for both (price, warranty, reviews) and check multiple sources.
